schemaSha256Note: re-hashed 2026-07-11 over the carried studiobox schema/ bundle (renamed identifiers; limabox baseline was 617920c8753001a23873f850934fd2a6ebb3b964f81f4ce4a7504ab6d92fbe48)

toolchain.commitNote: one commit ahead of the 0.1.0 release-prep commit c266609; capnpc-deno is not shipped in the JSR package, so the sibling checkout is the codegen toolchain of record

codegen.probeStatus: regenerated at the studiobox M1 gate on 2026-07-11; byte-identical to the committed src/wire/generated/ bindings and typechecks strict against the published runtime
codegen.observedAgainst: capnp-deno checkout ad0791189c781c84e636a55425da05ea3377114b; upstream fix c7f33fb (declaring interface ids + barrel merge) and c59dcde (window-atomic stream sender) are included, the cross-file lowering class is NOT
blockers: cross-file struct/enum references do not typecheck: field types are emitted as bare unimported TypeScript names (SbxError, EmptyResult, TransferCommit, FinishResult, ProtocolOffer, KeyValue, HandshakeResult, AuthResult, ReadResult; 159 TS2304 errors) and their runtime descriptors are lowered to TYPE_ANY_POINTER (83 fields across host_control, sandbox_agent, streams, supervisor)

cross-file interface references lose the generated service type and surface as raw CapabilityPointer (e.g. sandbox_agent SpawnParams.output for Streams.OutputSink) instead of typed stubs, while same-file interfaces get RpcStub<T>

the merged mod.ts barrel collides: every generated *_meta.ts exports the same five names (schemaFileId, schemaFilename, schemaImports, schemaNodes, interfaceMethods), a hard TS2308 for ANY two schemas, and 96 further method param/result and domain type names collide across modules (PingParams x4, AuthenticateParams x3, NegotiateParams x3, UsageResult, ProbeResult, KillResults, ...)
concurrent StreamSender.send maxInFlight race: addressed upstream by c59dcde before 0.1.0; qualified by the M1 streaming gate, not by this codegen gate
codegen.blockerNote: per-interface client/server/register/service-token artifacts DO generate for all 24 schema interfaces; the gap is confined to cross-file type lowering and barrel namespacing. tests/unit/capnp/cross_file_roundtrip_test.ts proves the published runtime codecs round-trip the cross-file composite wire shape, isolating the gap to the emitter. tools/check_wire.ts re-verifies the blocked status on every toolchain-present run and fails when upstream fixes land, forcing the full bindings to be committed.

compiledProbe.runtime: jsr:@nullstyle/capnp@0.1.0 (WASM session core embedded via the package's static import of generated/capnp_deno.wasm, 215990 bytes)

targets.aarch64-apple-darwin: executed: PROBE_OK (native; also with empty DENO_DIR + dead proxy, proving WASM embedding)
targets.aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu: executed: PROBE_OK inside the fc-smoke Lima VM (Ubuntu, kernel 6.8, Deno 2.9.1 artifact) with empty DENO_DIR
targets.x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu: compile-only on this host; execution proof lands with x86_64 CI (see PLAN M12)

compiledProbe.vsockFlagVerdict: Deno 2.9.x requires --unstable-vsock for Deno.listen/Deno.connect with transport 'vsock' (probed on guest Deno 2.9.2 aarch64-linux with vsock_loopback: no flag and --unstable-net both fail with 'The --unstable-vsock flag must be provided'); studioboxd and its in-guest tests must pass --unstable-vsock until the API stabilizes
